name: Sweet and Sour Barbecue Sauce
directions: Mix all ingredients, stir well.
Use for basting, or serve with----chicken--ribs--or lamb.

name: Sweet and Sassy Sauce
directions: Combine all ingredients in 8 cup glass measure cup and microwave, uncovered on high for 5- 6 minutes.
Good on meatballs and chicken.

name: 5-Ingredient Sweet & Sour Sauce
directions: Combine all ingredients.
(For double recipe only use 1/3 cup vinegar).
Cook for 3-5 minutes, until thick.

name: Bob's Spaghetti Sauce
directions: Brown chuck.
Add onions and green pepper and saute until onions are clear.
Add remaining ingredients.
Simmer 4-5 hours (it really does need this entire time for flavors to develop).
If sauce becomes bitter,.
add 4 tbs. BROWN sugar (less if desired).
Freezes well.
I've found lately that some of the cans/jars are not the same sizes as this recipe.
It makes absolutely no difference.
Use whatever is closest to the sizes called for.

name: Chicken Tender Dip
directions: Mix ingredients together.
Serve and enjoy!

name: Pammy's "1/3" Barbecue Sauce
directions: I put everything in my mini chopper and"chop" until mixed well.
Enjoy.

name: BBQ Sauce
directions: Place oil in saucepan and add onion and garlic over a low heat and cook until softened.
Add all other ingredients and bring to the boil, reduce heat and simmer for 5-8 minutes until thickened.
Set aside to cool and if you prefer smooth sauce, blend the sauce or you can leave it chunky.

name: Mustard Sauce / Dip
directions: Put the ingredients in the order listed into the top pot of a double boiler and whisk with a wire whisk to break the eggs.
Set the bottom pot of the double boiler with water on stove to boil.  When water is boiling turn down the heat a little and put the top pot into the water.
Cook the mustard over the boiling water for about 5-8 minutes stirring occasionally until the mixture coats the back of spoon.
Pour into glass jar and keep in the fridge.

name: Honey-Garlic Barbecue Sauce
directions: Heat oil in a saucepan over medium heat; add in onion and saute for about 3-4 minutes.
Add in garlic, paprika and oregano and saute for 1 minute.
Add in all remaining ingredients and simmer uncovered on low heat for 25-30 minutes, stirring occasionally with a wooden spoon.
Season with white salt and black pepper to taste.
Cool to room temperature, cover and refrigerate for 24 hours to blend flavors.
Stir well before using.
Store any leftover sauce tightly covered in the refrigerator.

name: Bamboozled Husband's Barbecue  Sauce
directions: Combine ketchup, HALF of beer, vinegar, cilantro, brown sugar, Worcestershire sauce, garlic, cumin, salt, Tabasco in medium sauce pan.  Simmer 1/2 hour.
When husband comes in and asks, "What smells so good?" mention the honey do list and hand him one cold beer.
When is list is complete, or at least a valiant effort has been made, serve sauce on chicken, beef, or pork.  Best if brushed on during grilling and served on side of cooked meat.

name: My Own Tartar Sauce"(Seafood Sauce)
directions: ALWAYS KEEP WELL CHILLED! Mix all ingredients together and let rest in the fridge for at least 10 minutes. My kids love this sauce tossed with cold pasta and black olives, and coarsely ground black pepper.You can add shrimp or chicken,parsley,basil etc!Try it as a twist on our classic tuna sandwich. Even Miracle Whip fans love it!

name: Tangy-Sweet Dijon Dipping Sauce
directions: Mix all ingredients together.
Benefits from sitting a few hours to blend flavors, but is also great right after being mixed.
Dip, enjoy!

name: Teriyaki Sauce & Glaze
directions: Combine ingredients in a saucepan on medium heat until the sugar is dissolved.
To make glaze: Mix 1/2 tablespoon cornstarch with 1 tablespoon water. Heat sauce on medium high heat and add cornstarch water mixture. Simmer until thickened about 1 minute.

name: Easy Cheesy Sauce
directions: Combine soup with milk & mustard powder in a small saucepan & stir over heat until boiling, simmer for 5 minutes.
Remove from heat & stir in cheese.

name: Teriyaki Sauce
directions: Mix ingredients well.
Marinate chicken, beef, seafood in sauce for as long as possible.
Cook on charcoal grill until done depending on cut of meat.  (Also does well in the broiler).
Use excess sauce for basting.

name: Chinese Pink Sauce
directions: Combine sugar, vinegar and ground dry mustard; mix well.
Add mayo, horseradish and ketchup, whip until smooth;
cover and let set overnight. Enjoy!

name: Beef Kabob Sauce
directions: Melt the butter first.
Combine the rest of ingredients saving the ketchup for last.
Stir together and simmer.
For a spicier sauce add some tobasco sauce.  Be careful not to overpower the sauce.

name: Honey and Sherry Stir-Fry Sauce
directions: Mix up your sauce ingredients
Add it to the stir-fry for the last minute of cooking.

name: Sour Cream Dill Sauce
directions: Combine all ingredients. Before adding sugar you may want to give it a taste!  Add sugar to your families taste.
Cover and chill until ready to serve.

name: Refined Steak Marinade
directions: In a medium saucepan, on low heat, melt butter to liquid and add golden brown sugar. Dissolve the sugar. Remove from heat.
Quickly add honey and mix well til blended.
Add all the other ingredients, mixing and blending well.
Return to low heat and stir for 5 minutes. Remove from heat.

Option A: Pour marinade into 2 ziploc bags and add steaks. Refrigerate overnight. Option B: In a rectangular Tupperware dish, pour half the marinade. Add the steaks, side by side, then pour the rest of the marinade on top of the steaks. Secure the lid on the dish and refrigerate overnight.

name: Thai Peanut Sauce
directions: In small saucepan combine all ingredients.
Heat until sugar is dissolved, stirring frequently.

name: Chicken Finger Dipping Sauce
directions: Combine all ingredients and whisk until all is incorporated.
Refrigerate after use.

name: Peanut Sauce
directions: Chop nuts very fine.
Combine all ingredients in small saucepan.
Simmer on low about 5minutes, stirring frequently.

name: Really Easy Tartar Sauce
directions: Mix together and serve with Recipe #406766 and fries.

name: Creamy Mustard Dipping Sauce
directions: Combine all ingredients in a small bowl, stir until well mixed. Store in an airtight container in the refrigerator.

name: Honey Mustard Mayonnaise
directions: Place all ingredients in a small bowl, and whisk until combined.
Serve as a dipper for chicken, or a dressing for meats or salads!

name: Tartar Sauce Seasoning Mix
directions: NOTES: WHEN MAKING TARTAR SAUCE I ADD A 50/50 BLEND OF BOTH DILL PICKLE RELISH OR CHOPPED DILL PICKLE, AND SWEET PICKLE RELISH OR CHOPPED SWEET PICKLE, PLUS A SMALL AMOUNT OF FRESH MINCED ONION. FOR A REDUCED FAT AND CALORIE TARTAR SAUCE, YOU CAN SUBSTITUTE MAYONNAISE WITH PLAIN LOWFAT OR NONFAT YOGURT (SEE PHOTO). LET YOUR OWN TASTEBUDS DECIDE UPON ANY OR NONE OF THE OPTIONAL CREATIVE ADDITIONS IN STEP #8.
MEASURE all ingredients into a small resealable container.
SEAL the container.
SHAKE well until thoroughly combined.
STORE seasoning in an airtight container away from light, heat and moisture.
USE 1 teaspoon Tartar Sauce Seasoning Mix with each 1/2 cup mayonnaise (adjust to taste).

TARTAR SAUCE RECIPE: In a small bowl, mix 1/2 cup mayonnaise, 2-4 tablespoons pickle relish (or minced pickle), and 1 teaspoon Tartar Sauce Seasoning Mix (or to taste); REFRIGERATE and allow minced onion to reconstitute and flavors to meld, approximately 15-30 minutes.

OPTIONAL CREATIVE ADDITIONS (KEEP IT SIMPLE): Buttermilk, capers, fresh chives, fresh cilantro, fresh dill, horseradish sauce, fresh lemon juice, fresh lemon zest, malt vinegar, fresh onion, fresh parsley, fresh ground pepper, fresh tarragon, Worcestershire sauce, plain yogurt.
SERVE with battered fried fish, cooked fresh fish, crab cakes, crab salad, croquettes, deviled eggs, hush puppies, as a sandwich spread or in tuna/seafood salad instead of plain mayonnaise.
ENJOY!

name: Kicked up Burger/Slider BBQ Sauce
directions: Mix the BBQ and mayo together and then mix all the rest of the ingredients together.  You may want to play with the amount of garlic and lime juice.   Also, I've made this with adobo sauce for extra heat and it came out great.

name: A Very Popular BBQ Sauce
directions: In a blender, combine brown sugar, ketchup, vinegar, water and Worcestershire sauce.  Season with mustard, paprika, salt, pepper and hot pepper sauce.  Blend until smooth.
FOOTNOTE:
This will fill a 32-ounce ketchup bottle exactly.  Cut back on the brown sugar to make it less sweet, or add more Worcestershire sauce for more 'steak sauce' appeal.

name: Yogurt and Dill "Crack" Sauce
directions: Combine all ingredients well.
Enjoy the crack sauce!

name: General Tso's Sauce
directions: Combine all ingredients in a small sauce pot and heat over medium heat.
Once the sauce comes to a boil, turn it down and allow to simmer until ready to serve.
